methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
General Galactic is a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Halen Mattison and Luke Neise that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private frontier-tech peers in the same sector at comparable or adjacent stages, alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA), including reference to companies like TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a proxy valuation baseline and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $25M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—producing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulation across both methods produces a low estimate of $21M, a median of $88M, and a high of $172M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation anchors in early-stage frontier energy, where peer companies at Series A command valuations 11–20x larger than comparable capital raised. Key uncertainties include General Galactic's specific technology differentiation relative to the peer set, path to Series A deployment, and whether comparable peers' elevated valuations reflect sector momentum or concentration of capital among leading projects.
